steps.title: Set up the factorization and find the other factor
steps.content:
If $(x + 2b)$ is a factor, the quadratic must factor as $(x + 2b)(x + r)$ for some value $r$. Expanding:
$$(x + 2b)(x + r) = x^2 + (r + 2b)x + 2br$$
All choices have constant term $18b$. Setting $2br = 18b$ and dividing by the positive constant $b$:
$$2r = 18$$
$$r = 9$$

steps.title: Find which choice gives a positive integer b
steps.content:
With $r = 9$, the expression expands to $x^2 + (9 + 2b)x + 18b$. So the middle coefficient must equal $9 + 2b$. For each choice, solve for $b$:
  • A: $9 + 2b = 9$ → $b = 0$ ✗ (not positive)
  • B: $9 + 2b = 24$ → $b = 7.5$ ✗ (not an integer)
  • C: $9 + 2b = 30$ → $b = 10.5$ ✗ (not an integer)
  • D: $9 + 2b = 39$ → $b = 15$ ✓
D is correct.
